title: node-postgres Pools, Queries, Transactions, and Failure Semantics · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A backend pairing room predicts runtime, request, middleware, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract for node-postgres pools, queries, transactions, and failure semantics. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causa…
objectives: Create one bounded pg Pool per service role with connection, idle, acquisition, maximum-lifetime, error, statistics, readiness, and pool.end behavior.
Use parameterized values, allowlisted identifiers, explicit columns, row validation, cardinality checks, timeouts, and cancellation without SQL string concatenation.
Acquire and release a client exactly once with finally while distinguishing acquisition, query, protocol, server, timeout, cancellation, and application failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pg Pool Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 4608 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify pool, transaction, release, and ambiguous-commit evidence without blind replay; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pg Pool Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create one bounded pg pool per service role with connection, idle, acquisition, maximum-lifetime, error, statistics, readiness, and pool.end behavior. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Pg Pool Lifecycle, keep Pg Pool Lifecycle connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

hints: For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Pg Ambiguous Commit, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Pg Ambiguous Commit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Pg Ambiguous Commit, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team handles case 3653 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify pool, transaction, release, and ambiguous-commit evidence without blind replay; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify known commit, known rollback, and unknown commit outcomes and reconcile by operation identity instead of replaying uncertain effects.

For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Pg Ambiguous Commit, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: A connection error during COMMIT proves the transaction rolled back and is safe to retry.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Pg Ambiguous Commit condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Pg Ambiguous Commit output and hide the violated bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Pg Ambiguous Commit.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Pg Ambiguous Commit happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Pg Ambiguous Commit for bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Pg Ambiguous Commit
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Pg Ambiguous Commit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop team handles case 1320 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify pool, transaction, release, and ambiguous-commit evidence without blind replay; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then lower a byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs at its owner. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Classify known commit, known rollback, and unknown commit outcomes and reconcile by operation identity instead of replaying uncertain effects.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Pg Ambiguous Commit.
steps.why: Retrieving Pg Ambiguous Commit through the concrete bounded pg repository and transaction outcome contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Pg Ambiguous Commit, keep Pg Ambiguous Commit connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Classify known commit, known rollback, and unknown commit outcomes and reconcile by operation identity instead of replaying uncertain effects.
